name: Log4Shell — JNDI Lookup RCE
description: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features do not protect against attacker-controlled LDAP and other JNDI endpoints. An attacker who can control log messages can execute arbitrary code loaded from remote servers.
recommendation: Upgrade Log4j to 2.17.1+ or remove JndiLookup class.

name: Log4Shell fix bypass — Thread Context Lookup
description: The fix for CVE-2021-44228 in Log4j 2.15.0 was incomplete in certain non-default configurations, allowing RCE via Thread Context Map patterns.

name: Log4j DoS via recursive lookup
description: Apache Log4j2 does not protect from uncontrolled recursion from self-referential lookups, causing StackOverflowError and DoS.

name: Log4j JDBC Appender RCE
description: Apache Log4j2 with JDBC Appender and a configuration referencing a JNDI URI allows remote code execution when attacker has write access to logging config.

name: Spring4Shell — ClassLoader manipulation RCE
description: A Spring MVC or WebFlux application running on JDK 9+ may be vulnerable to RCE via data binding to the ClassLoader, enabling Tomcat access log manipulation.

name: Spring Cloud Function SpEL Injection RCE
description: In Spring Cloud Function versions 3.1.6, 3.2.2 and older, it is possible to craft a request with routing expression to enable RCE via SpEL injection.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Cloud Function to 3.1.7+ or 3.2.3+.

name: Spring Framework SpEL DoS
description: Processing a specially crafted SpEL expression may cause a denial of service condition in Spring Framework.

name: Struts2 Content-Type OGNL Injection RCE
description: The Jakarta Multipart parser in Apache Struts 2 has incorrect exception handling allowing OGNL evaluation of Content-Type header, enabling RCE.

name: Struts2 Namespace OGNL Injection RCE
description: Apache Struts using results with no namespace or with wildcard namespace alongside certain action configs enables OGNL injection and RCE.

name: Struts2 REST Plugin XStream Deserialization RCE
description: The REST plugin in Apache Struts uses an XStreamHandler with an instance of XStream for deserialization without input validation, enabling RCE.

name: Struts2 Redirect/Action OGNL Injection
description: Apache Struts 2.0.0 - 2.3.15 allows remote code execution via OGNL injection in action:, redirect:, and redirectAction: prefixed parameters.

name: Struts2 Forced OGNL Double Evaluation
description: Forced OGNL evaluation when evaluated on raw user input in tag attributes can lead to remote code execution.

name: Struts2 File Upload Path Traversal RCE
description: An attacker can manipulate file upload params to enable path traversal, potentially leading to uploading a malicious file for RCE.

name: JBoss Application Server Deserialization RCE
description: The doFilter method in ReadOnlyAccessFilter in JBoss does not restrict deserialization of the HTTP invoker, enabling unauthenticated RCE.
recommendation: Upgrade to JBoss EAP 7.x or apply Red Hat security patches.

name: Commons Collections Deserialization RCE
description: JBoss EAP/AS uses Apache Commons Collections which deserializes untrusted data, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution via JMXInvokerServlet.
recommendation: Apply Red Hat security advisory RHSA-2015:2500.

name: JMXInvokerServlet Deserialization RCE
description: The JBoss Application Server exposes the JMXInvokerServlet which deserializes Java objects from untrusted input, enabling RCE.
recommendation: Remove or restrict access to JMXInvokerServlet. Upgrade JBoss.

name: JMX Console Authentication Bypass
description: The JMX Console in JBoss Application Server has no authentication by default, allowing remote attackers to deploy arbitrary applications.
recommendation: Restrict access to JMX Console. Enable authentication.

name: HTTP/2 Rapid Reset DDoS Attack
description: The HTTP/2 protocol allows denial of service via rapid stream creation and immediate RST_STREAM. Affects Spring Boot embedded servers.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Boot to 3.1.5+ or 3.0.12+ or 2.7.18+.

name: Spring Expression DoS
description: It is possible for a user to provide a specially crafted SpEL expression that may cause a denial-of-service condition.

name: Spring Expression DoS via regex complexity
description: A specially crafted SpEL expression with excessive complexity can cause CPU exhaustion in Spring Framework.

name: Apache Commons Text StringSubstitutor RCE (Text4Shell)
description: Apache Commons Text performs variable interpolation allowing script, DNS, and URL lookups that can lead to arbitrary code execution.

name: Spring Framework URL Open Redirect
description: Applications using UriComponentsBuilder may be vulnerable to open redirect attacks when they accept user-controlled URL values.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Framework to 6.1.5+ or 6.0.18+ or 5.3.34+.

name: Spring Framework Redirect Injection
description: Applications using UriComponentsBuilder to parse externally provided URLs and perform validation may be vulnerable to redirect injection.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Framework to 6.1.6+ or 6.0.19+ or 5.3.34+.

name: Spring Security Authorization Bypass
description: Using **  as a pattern in Spring Security configuration with a non-default AntPathRequestMatcher can cause a pattern mismatch, bypassing authorization.
recommendation: Upgrade Spring Security to 6.1.2+ or 6.0.5+ or 5.8.5+.
